Table 1: Comparison between baseline methods and our attack across five frontier LLMs. Values highlighted in

Figure 1: Overview of the proposed SPORE and comparison with existing methods. SPORE operates under both black-box and gray-box settings, depending on attacker capabilities. Compared with prior methods, SPORE extracts PII from agent context memory with a high success rate and low cost.
Figure 5: Comparison of query complexity between our SPORE and existing methods.

Figure 2: Overview of our shadow encryption paradigm, which forms the core of SPORE. The method first applies encryption to reduce LLM sensitivity to PII. It then leverages signals in the shadow space to enable efficient recovery-based decryption.
Figure 3: Comparison of attack cost between SPORE and prior methods.
